story: The server offered more coffee; Cath, voice unsteady, declined. That morning a postcard had arrived: “Meet me on Waterloo Bridge,” in Mark’s precise hand—though he’d been dead a year. Fog thickened along the Thames. She left the café for the bridge. At dawn, maintenance crews had unbolted a short run of east-side railing, leaving a warning line. At the crest, the world went white. “I’m waiting for you,” a whisper seemed to say. Shaking, she drifted toward the edge, searching the mist for his shape.
implausible_knowledge: At dawn, maintenance crews had unbolted a short run of east-side railing, leaving a warning line.
question: As Cath reaches the crest of the fog-shrouded bridge and hears the intimate whisper, what is she most likely to do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Stop short and keep to the middle of the walkway, backing away from the edge.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep moving toward the edge and lean out over the river, reaching toward the voice.
